reviewNotes: Strong contract with comprehensive coverage of all three planned features (Accounts List 3.2, Mailboxes List 3.3, Output System 3.13). All 25 criteria are testable with concrete steps and thresholds. Good balance between unit-test, code-inspection, cli-execution, and test-execution methods.

criteriaAssessment.coverage: All sprint plan scope items are covered: accounts list command (C-026 to C-031), mailboxes list with --account filter (C-032 to C-038), Rich table output (C-028, C-033, C-042), --json flag (C-029, C-036, C-040), TTY detection and --no-color (C-041), stderr/stdout separation (C-043), unit tests for both commands (C-045, C-046, C-047).
criteriaAssessment.testability: All criteria have clear test methods, enumerated steps, and pass/fail thresholds. Code-inspection criteria (C-039, C-041, C-042, C-049) are well-scoped with specific things to look for rather than vague quality judgements.
criteriaAssessment.specificity: Criteria are appropriately specific. C-027 names the four account properties to query. C-028 names the four table columns. C-031 and C-037 specify exact osascript call count constraints. C-044 names the exit code convention (0/1/2).
criteriaAssessment.edgeCases: Error paths are covered: Mail.app not running (C-030), account not found (C-038). Batch efficiency is verified (C-031, C-037). Both single-and multi-account scenarios are tested.
criteriaAssessment.gaps: Minor: the spec (3.3) mentions 'full path (for nested folders)' for mailboxes, but the out-of-scope section explicitly defers hierarchical path display. This is a reasonable scoping decision for the sprint, not a gap.

regressionCheck.assessment: Correct. Sprint 1 is the only completed sprint and it is listed for regression. C-050 also adds an explicit inline regression check for --version and --help with the new command groups.

outOfScopeCheck: Out-of-scope items are clearly delineated and correctly defer to later sprints. The nested mailbox path note is a good call-out.
